signals.headline: Ethereum Price Surge on Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz Reopening | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Price Surge on Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz Reopening | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Ethereum (ETH) jumped 6.36% to $2,464.19 on April 17, 2026, breaking the $2,400 resistance amid broader crypto rally triggered by the confirmed Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, vaporizing prior resistance in hours.[3]  
ETH opened at $2,348.49 (down 0.5%
matched_markets: Will Jared Kushner have a diplomatic meeting with Iran by April 30?
Will 40 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day by April 30?
Ethereum Up or Down - April 18, 6:45PM-6:50PM ET

signals.headline: AMRO forecasted **2
signals.raw:
AMRO forecasted **2.3%** rise (up 0.4pp)[6].

No new official data (e.g., April CPI, typically released mid-month) or policy shifts confirmed since these; next inflation release expected end-April/early May, per patterns[2].

signals.headline: Bank of Korea held rates at 2
signals.raw: Bank of Korea held rates at 2.5% (early April meeting), noting rising price pressures likely exceeding February's 2.2% forecast, with data-dependent outlook amid uncertainty[4].

signals.headline: NO** | **LOW** confidence in market-moving positive developments, as sources confirm stalemate over Strait of Hormuz, nu
signals.raw: NO** | **LOW** confidence in market-moving positive developments, as sources confirm stalemate over Strait of Hormuz, nuclear demands, and broader issues, with no new resolutions since April 11-12.[2][3][4] One report hints at back-channel progress toward a framework, but it lacks specifics or confirmation and predates the breakdown.[7]
matched_markets: Will 40 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day by April 30?

signals.headline: Major investment banks raised their 2026 South Korea inflation forecasts to an average of **2
signals.raw: Major investment banks raised their 2026 South Korea inflation forecasts to an average of **2.4%** (up 0.4pp from February), driven by high oil prices, weak won, and Middle East energy shocks; JP Morgan and Citigroup see April at ~**2.6-2.8%**, potentially exceeding 3% later[1].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: March 2026 actual inflation rose to **2
signals.raw: March 2026 actual inflation rose to **2.2%** (from 2.0% prior), below market expectations of 2.4%[2].

signals.headline: IMF on April 14 forecasted **2
signals.raw: IMF on April 14 forecasted **2.5%** for 2026 (up 0.7pp from prior)[3][5].
